difficulty.reason: Perennial, division W16, edible purple flowers, pest deterrent

growth_stages.name: Dormant
growth_stages.look_for: Brown dried grass-like tufts from last year

growth_stages.name: Spring Growth
growth_stages.look_for: Thin green tubes shooting up from each division

prompt.en: Snip a few green tubes with scissors -- taste them! Mild onion flavor!
prompt.da: Klip et par groenne roer med saksen -- smag dem! Mild loegsmag!

growth_stages.name: Flowering
growth_stages.look_for: Purple globe-shaped flowers on top of stems

prompt.en: Pull apart a purple ball and taste the tiny flowers -- onion confetti!
prompt.da: Pil en lilla kugle fra hinanden og smag de smaa blomster -- loeg-konfetti!

growth_stages.name: Summer Growth
growth_stages.look_for: Regrowth after flower cutting, continuous green tubes

prompt.en: Snip a handful for dinner -- chop them up for the baked potato!
prompt.da: Klip en haandfuld til aftensmad -- hak dem til den bagte kartoffel!

human_readable.en: Water regularly but tolerates some drought. Keep reasonably moist.
human_readable.da: Vand regelmaessigt, men taaler lidt toerke. Hold rimelig fugtig.

harvest.notes: Perennial: cut-and-come-again herb. Purple flowers edible May-June. Repels Japanese beetles and aphids.

companion_plants.notes: Chives repel Japanese beetles and aphids from raspberries. Alliums inhibit legume nitrogen fixation.

message.en: Chives could use a drink -- they look a bit droopy.
message.da: Purloeg kunne bruge en taar vand -- de ser lidt slappe ud.
